UKULIMA SACCO SOCIETY LIMITED
LOAN POLICY DOCUMENT
Revised: January 2024

INTRODUCTION
Ukulima SACCO Society Limited is a member-owned financial cooperative
registered under the Co-operative Societies Act, Cap 490 of the Laws of Kenya.
This loan policy governs all lending activities of the SACCO and applies to
all members seeking credit facilities from the society.

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
A member must have been active for at least six months to qualify for any loan.
Members must have a minimum share capital of KES 5,000 at the time of application.
Members must have no outstanding disciplinary issues with the SACCO.
A member must have made consistent monthly contributions for the preceding three months.
Members with existing loans in arrears are not eligible for additional credit.

LOAN LIMITS AND TYPES
The maximum loan amount is three times the member's total deposits and share capital.
Emergency loans shall not exceed KES 50,000 and must be repaid within six months.
Development loans shall not exceed KES 500,000 and carry a repayment period of 60 months.
School fees loans shall not exceed KES 150,000 per academic year.
Business loans are available up to KES 1,000,000 subject to business appraisal.

INTEREST RATES
Emergency loans attract an interest rate of 1% per month on reducing balance.
Development loans attract an interest rate of 1.2% per month on reducing balance.
School fees loans attract an interest rate of 1% per month on reducing balance.
Business loans attract an interest rate of 1.5% per month on reducing balance.
Interest rates are subject to review by the Board of Directors annually.

GUARANTOR REQUIREMENTS
Every loan above KES 10,000 requires a minimum of two guarantors.
Guarantors must be active SACCO members with no existing loan defaults.
A member may not guarantee more than three active loans simultaneously.
Guarantors must sign a guarantee form acknowledging their liability.
The combined deposits and share capital of guarantors must equal or exceed the loan amount.

DEFAULT AND PENALTIES
A member is considered in default after two consecutive missed repayments.
A penalty of 5% per month shall be charged on the outstanding defaulted amount.
The SACCO reserves the right to liquidate guarantor assets after 90 days of default.
Defaulting members shall be suspended from accessing further credit facilities.
Legal action may be instituted against members who default for more than 180 days.
The cost of debt recovery, including legal fees, shall be borne by the defaulting member.

LOAN DISBURSEMENT
Approved loans shall be disbursed within five working days of approval.
Disbursement shall be made directly to the member's registered bank account or M-Pesa.
The SACCO shall deduct a processing fee of 1% of the loan amount before disbursement.
Members must sign a loan agreement form before disbursement is effected.

AMENDMENTS
This policy may be amended by the Board of Directors as necessary.
Members shall be notified of any material changes at least 30 days in advance.
All amendments are subject to ratification at the next Annual General Meeting.
